history.title: HISTORY
history.footer: The city remembers itself in fragments.

history.moments.sentence: A ford becomes a center. Movement comes before capital.
Power chooses a place. Paris becomes a name, not yet a form.
Royalty settles in. The temporary begins to last.
Notre-Dame begins. The city looks upward.
Philippe Auguste closes Paris. To protect is already to exist.
The papacy arrives in Paris. The spiritual turns political.
The city turns against itself. Faith fractures space.
The street speaks. Power learns to listen to the crowd.
A piece of furniture becomes a tribune. The private becomes public.
The city governs itself. Too early, too strong.
Speech spills everywhere. Nothing contains meaning anymore.
The city hesitates. It awaits a new gesture.
